article.body:
In the evolving landscape of AI automation, the focus has shifted from simple task execution to intelligent planning. The concept of Planning AI Needs Memory, Not Just Automation highlights a critical shift: memory is not just a chat history—it's a structured operational context that drives real business outcomes. This guide walks you through building a practical AI automation workflow that incorporates memory, approval steps, and structured operational context for measurable impact.

Key Takeaways

  • AI memory should capture structured operational context, not just chat history.
  • Start with a Slack intake workflow, CRM follow-up workflow, or support triage workflow.
  • Implement approval steps and quality checks to ensure accuracy.
  • Measure success with KPIs like first response time, resolution time, and manual hours saved.
  • Avoid treating AI memory as just chat history and ignoring approval steps.

What is Planning AI Needs Memory, Not Just Automation?

The concept emphasizes that AI should not just automate tasks but also remember and learn from past events to make better decisions. In logistics and supply chain planning, this means capturing structured operational context and associating events with the entities that matter. As noted in Logistics Viewpoints, a useful memory layer should go beyond chat history to capture structured operational context.

Business Use Case: AI-Powered Support Triage Workflow

Consider a logistics company that receives hundreds of daily support requests. By implementing an AI-powered support triage workflow, the company can automatically categorize, prioritize, and route tickets based on structured memory of past interactions. This reduces manual effort and improves response times.

Workflow Steps

  1. Intake: Support requests are received via Slack or email.
  2. Memory Retrieval: AI retrieves relevant past interactions and context from a structured memory layer.
    3. n
  3. Categorization: AI categorizes the request based on memory and business rules.
  4. Approval: High-priority or uncertain cases are escalated to a human operator for approval.
  5. Routing: Tickets are automatically routed to the appropriate team or individual.
  6. Logging: All interactions are logged for future reference and audit.

Tools Needed

  • Slack for intake
  • CRM for follow-up
  • AI platform (e.g., OpenAI) for memory and decision-making
  • Zapier or Make for workflow automation

Measuring Success

  • First response time
  • Resolution time
  • Manual hours saved
  • Customer satisfaction

Common Mistakes

  • Treating AI memory as just chat history
  • Ignoring approval steps
  • Not capturing structured operational context

Implementation Checklist

  • Define the workflow steps and handoff points
  • Set up AI memory to capture structured operational context
  • Implement approval steps for uncertain cases
  • Log all interactions for audit and future reference
  • Measure success with KPIs like first response time, resolution time, and manual hours saved

Troubleshooting and Failure Handling

  • API timeout: Implement retry logic and alerting for API failures.
  • Low-confidence AI output: Escalate to human approval for uncertain cases.
  • Duplicate tickets: Use deduplication logic to prevent duplicate tickets.
  • Human approval handling: Implement approval steps for high-priority or uncertain cases.

What to do next

Start by implementing a simple Slack intake workflow, CRM follow-up workflow, or support triage workflow to demonstrate the value of AI memory in capturing structured operational context. Measure success with KPIs like first response time, resolution time, and manual hours saved. Avoid treating AI memory as just chat history and ignoring approval steps.

FAQ

  • What is Planning AI Needs Memory, Not Just Automation - Logistics Viewpoints? It's a concept that emphasizes the importance of memory in AI planning, not just automation, to capture structured operational context and associate events with the entities that matter in supply chain planning.
  • When should a business use it? When they want to implement AI automation workflows that go beyond simple automation by incorporating memory, approval steps, and structured operational context for real business impact.
  • What workflow should be automated first? Start with a Slack intake workflow, CRM follow-up workflow, or support triage workflow to demonstrate the value of AI memory in capturing structured operational context.
  • What mistakes should be avoided? Avoid treating AI memory as just chat history, ignoring approval steps, and not capturing structured operational context.

CTA

Ready to implement AI automation workflows that go beyond simple automation by incorporating memory, approval steps, and structured operational context for real business impact? Book an automation consult to get started.
